name: Papaya Liqueur
recipeInstructions:
1 Look for medium-sized papaya (slightly larger than a pear) with smooth, unbruised skin and a fruity aroma. The best time to use this recipe is May and June.
2 Check the stem for decay or softness. Dark spots on the skin are a bad sign and will cause a bad flavor.
3 Cut the papaya in half, remove the seeds, and peel off the skin.
4 Cut the papaya into medium chunks.
5 Place the papaya pieces in a jar with vodka and lemon peel (twist the peel to release its flavor).
6 Let steep for 1 week.
7 Strain out the fruit pieces.
8 Squeeze the juice from the remaining fruit.
9 If desired, add sugar syrup to taste.
10 Store the liqueur in a quart jar for up to 3 weeks.
